You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 87153ae1).

## Issue to Resolve

Formatting enum class with member definition and missing trailing commas causes rule violations on first run
## Expected Behavior
<!---Tell us what should happen. -->
<!-- If relevant, provide a link to the relevant section in the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin Style Guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide).  -->

<!-- Note: Ktlint should not produce any code which conflict with IntelliJ default code formatting. So please verify that your expectation is accepted by IntelliJ default code formatting. -->

When formatting and validating the following enum class, the ktlint command should exit successfully (status `0`):

```kotlin
enum class TestEnum() {
    A,
    B;

    companion object {
        const val A_NUMBER = 1L
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
<!---Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- Provide the exact command which was executed but please -->
<!--- ensure that the flag "--log-level=debug" is added to the -->
<!--- command as well. Provide the output of this command. -->

When running ktlint on the above enum class, it is formatted correctly, but the run fails with rule violations (exit status `1`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format 
17:25:00.694 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:1:1: Missing trailing comma before "}" (trailing-comma-on-declaration-site)
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:5:4: Unnecessary semicolon (no-semi)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  no-semi: 1
  trailing-comma-on-declaration-site: 1
Exit status: 1 
```

I note that the line number for the `no-semi` violation is the line number after formatting.

A second run on the (now properly formatted) code runs correctly (exit status `0`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format
17:25:05.397 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
Exit status: 0
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run command on above enum class:
```ktlint --format```

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I have the same issue

See release notes of 0.48.0:
> * The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))

@paul-dingemans I don't quite follow. My issue is that after doing formatting, ktlint reports invalid  `no-semi` and `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` rule violations. A subsequent run of ktlint (correctly) passes the same code.

Ah now I see. Although it is not visual in the textual representation of the code, the trailing comma is not inserted at the correct place in the AST representation of the code. I will try to fix it.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `RELEASING.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 47→
 48→/**
 49→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50→ *
 51→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52→ */
 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
115→        node: ASTNode,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
118→    ) {
119→        val inspectNode = node
120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun visitValueList(
133→        node: ASTNode,
134→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146→                        emit = emit,
147→                    )
148→                }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun visitTypeList(
153→        node: ASTNode,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→    ) {
157→        val inspectNode = node
158→            .children()
159→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164→            emit = emit,
165→        )
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)

241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)

308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }

347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }

353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }

399→        /**

401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**

406→         */

411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**

416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,

The trailing comma insertion for enum entries with a semicolon is misplaced, causing AST mismatches and lint errors; the fix is to insert the trailing comma before the semicolon node, ensuring correct positioning in the AST and proper formatting.**Analyzing semicolon repositioning in enums**

The trailing comma rule moves the semicolon node out of the enum entry to the class body level, likely to maintain AST correctness, but this repositioning may confuse the no-semi rule and cause false unnecessary semicolon errors on first run; this indicates a need to refine when and how the semicolon is relocated during trailing comma insertion to avoid triggering no-semi violations.

The current logic for requiring a semicolon after enum entries appears flawed, as it checks if the semicolon is a child of the enum entry and last before class body closing—but in Kotlin PSI, the separating semicolon is likely a child of the class body, not the enum entry, especially when members follow. This affects how no-semicolon rules and trailing comma adjustments interact.**Identifying trailing comma rule bug moving semicolon**

The trailing comma rule mistakenly moves the semicolon out of the enum entry to be a sibling, causing the no-semicolon rule to flag it as unnecessary. The fix is to stop moving the semicolon from the enum entry and keep it as its child to align with no-semicolon logic and correct Kotlin PSI structure.

**Planning enum formatting adjustment**

The tests rely on trailing comma rule plus wrapping and indentation for enum formatting, not no-semi. To keep expected formatting with semicolons on their own line, we should avoid moving the semicolon to the parent but instead insert whitespace before it, preserving trailing comments on the enum entry line.**Refining semicolon placement inside enum entry**

This preserves formatting and satisfies the no-semi rule by making the semicolon a child of the enum entry.

 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :

 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,

 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),

 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→

 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),

122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(

168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177→            return
178→        }
179→
180→        val inspectNode = node
181→            .children()
182→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187→            emit = emit,
188→        )
189→    }
190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }

215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }

231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])

241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }

347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()

353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }

370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }

378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }

399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**

411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
